title: Fermented Snappy Slovakian Corn Roast
description: A jain-friendly, fermented, snappy Slovakian dish featuring dried white corn and roast, perfect for hangover cures.

ingredients.name: Roast beef or pork fat
ingredients.notes: Use leftover roast for best results.

ingredients.name: Salt
ingredients.notes: Use flaky sea salt for best flavor.

instructions: In a large bowl, combine dried corn, caraway seeds, salt, and black pepper.
Add water to the bowl and mix until corn is well coated.
Heat olive oil in a large roasting pan over medium-high heat. Add onion, garlic, and apples. Roast for 10 minutes, until onion is translucent.
Add corn mixture to the roasting pan and stir to combine with onion mixture.
Transfer roasting pan to the preheated oven and roast for 20 minutes, until corn is lightly browned.
Remove roasting pan from the oven and let cool slightly.
Use a spoon to break up corn into smaller pieces, creating a snappy texture.
Transfer corn mixture to a baking dish and cover with foil. Let ferment at room temperature for 24 hours.
After fermentation, stir in roast beef or pork fat and chopped parsley.
Serve warm, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
Refrigerate or freeze for later use.
